_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised autopilot, LOCAL track)
_meta.note: FL local-officials batch — three FL State Attorneys (Hillsborough/Lopez-R, Miami-Dade/Fernandez Rundle-D, Orange-Osceola/Worrell-D), Miami-Dade County Mayor Levine Cava-D, Miami-Dade Sheriff Cordero-Stutz-R. Roster verified June 2026 via Ballotpedia, ballotpedia.org, official sites, FL Politics, Miami Herald, Tampa Bay Times. Scores reset to null where no evidence; TRUE/FALSE only on documented votes, charging policies, EOs, statements, or formal endorsements. Surfacing HIGH-VALUE deviations: Lopez's Episcopal lay leadership + clean SA charging-policy reverse of suspended Warren; Levine Cava's anomalous +9% police budget posture under FY25-26 deficit; Fernandez Rundle's 30+ year non-prosecution of police killings (= institutional deference, NOT affirmative pro-police HIGH BAR — null, not TRUE); Cordero-Stutz's documented 287(g) MOA chain Feb 2025 + Trump endorsement; Worrell's DeSantis EO 23-160 suspension upheld 5-2 by FL Supreme Court 2024-06-06.

profile.religion: Lifelong member, St. John's Episcopal Church, Tampa; former Junior Warden of the Vestry (Osprey Observer)
profile.education: BA Middlebury College; JD Suffolk University; H.B. Plant HS (Tampa)
profile.background: State Attorney, 13th Judicial Circuit (Hillsborough County), appointed by Gov. DeSantis Aug 2022 after suspending Andrew Warren; won full 4-year term Nov 5, 2024 (52.7% vs. Warren); sworn in Jan 7, 2025. Next election 2028. Career prosecutor: former ASA, former Hillsborough County Court judge, former 2nd DCA attorney. Leads ~300-person office handling ~50,000 cases/yr. Endorsed by Pam Bondi (now US AG).

suzy-lopez.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Hillsborough SA13 since Jan 7, 2025 full term (DeSantis-appointed Aug 2022; won 52.7% Nov 2024 vs. Warren), term to Jan 2029. HARD evidence: (1) sanctity_of_life q1 TRUE — campaigned specifically on willingness to enforce FL abortion law that predecessor Warren refused; Warren's non-prosecution pledge was the trigger for DeSantis's 2022 suspension and Lopez's appointment (Fox 13, WMNF). (2) biblical_marriage q2 TRUE (indirect) — Warren also pledged not to prosecute gender-affirming care cases for minors; Lopez's appointment was explicitly tied to enforcing FL law as written (WUTV/Sinclair). (3) christian_liberty q0 TRUE — lifelong member of St. John's Episcopal Church Tampa, former Junior Warden of the Vestry (Osprey Observer profile) — public Christian affiliation + lay leadership; note Episcopal tradition is not evangelical-conservative on doctrine, so other Christian-liberty cells (q1-q4) remain null. (4) public_justice q0 TRUE (HIGH BAR met) — endorsed by FL PBA, Tampa PBA, Sun Coast PBA, West Central PBA, Tampa Bay Area Chiefs of Police Assn; personally champions strong PD partnerships via 'Enforce The Law' advisory board. (5) public_justice q1 TRUE — personally meets with homicide victims' families; 'crime victim advocacy should always remain in the forefront.' (6) public_justice q2 TRUE — filed pretrial-detention motions seeking no-bond hold for violent defendants (Foolio murder case). (7) public_justice q3 TRUE — aggressive fentanyl-trafficking prosecution; office stated it will 'vigorously prosecute drug dealers'; cited 1,300% jump in fentanyl cases. (8) public_justice q4 TRUE — sought death penalty within 24 hrs of appointment (Matthew Terry); sought DP repeatedly (Dover double-murder, dismemberment case). (9) border_immigration q2 TRUE — office lists fighting illegal immigration as a focus area; joined Protecting Americans Action Fund pro-enforcement coalition. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on personhood (q0), marriage definition (q0), trans-specific statement (q2), Pride engagement (q4), school choice / parental notification / SOGI K-12 (not SA jurisdiction), christian_liberty q1-q4, economic_stewardship q2, election_integrity, refuse_state_overreach (her stance is pro-state-law-enforcement, not resistance), border q1/q3, self_defense q0-q4 — those cells null. DEVIATION-FLAG: she explicitly self-frames as 'a prosecutor, not a politician' — limits how many policy cells fill TRUE.

1.note: Campaigned and took office specifically on willingness to enforce FL abortion law; said Warren 'essentially violated his oath' by pledging non-prosecution. Stands ready to prosecute illegal abortions under FL 6-week ban.

2.note: Appointment by DeSantis was explicitly tied to enforcing FL law as written — predecessor Warren had pledged not to prosecute gender-affirming-care cases for minors; Lopez took office committed to enforcement.

0.note: Lifelong member of St. John's Episcopal Church Tampa; former Junior Warden of the Vestry — documented public Christian affiliation and lay leadership.

Endorsed by FL PBA, Tampa PBA, Sun Coast PBA, West Central PBA, Tampa Bay Area Chiefs of Police Assn; personally champions PD partnerships via Enforce The Law advisory board — affirmative pro-police advocacy meets HIGH BAR.

1.note: Personally meets with homicide victims' families; published stance 'crime victim advocacy should always remain in the forefront.'

2.note: Filed pretrial-detention motions seeking no-bond hold for violent defendants (Foolio murder case) — affirmative anti-cashless-bail prosecutorial posture.

3.note: Aggressive fentanyl-trafficking prosecution; office stated it will 'vigorously prosecute drug dealers'; cited 1,300% jump in fentanyl cases — rejects public-health framing.

4.note: Sought death penalty within 24 hrs of appointment (Matthew Terry case); sought DP repeatedly (Dover double-murder, dismemberment) — affirmative anti-soft-on-crime.

2.note: Office lists fighting illegal immigration as a stated focus area; joined Protecting Americans Action Fund pro-enforcement coalition — anti-sanctuary prosecutorial posture.

profile.religion: Jewish — first Jewish Mayor of Miami-Dade County (JTA Jan 2021); no public profession of Christ; no documented anti-Christian-display action either
profile.education: BA Psychology, Yale 1977; JD Columbia 1981; MSW Columbia 1983
profile.background: Mayor of Miami-Dade County, Democrat. Elected 2020 (first female Miami-Dade mayor); re-elected outright in Aug 20, 2024 nonpartisan primary at 58% (general election cancelled). Current term to Nov 21, 2028. Prior: Miami-Dade County Commissioner 2014-2020; founded Catalyst Miami (Human Services Coalition). Born Sept 14, 1955, NYC. Husband Robert Cava (physician); two children incl. one openly gay child (motivation cited for LGBTQ advocacy).

daniella-levine-cava.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Miami-Dade Mayor since Nov 2020, re-elected outright Aug 2024 primary 58%, term to Nov 21, 2028 (Ballotpedia, FL Politics). HARD evidence: (1) sanctity_of_life q0 FALSE — campaigned for FL Amendment 4 abortion-rights amendment ('Our Bodies Our Lives' rally, Miami Times). (2) sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE — 'strongly opposed' parental-consent-for-minor-abortion legislation (FL Politics). (3) biblical_marriage q2 FALSE — at Pride flag-raising: 'We say gay. We say trans.'; launched county Community ID program serving transgender residents (FIU SFMN, WSVN). (4) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — first Miami-Dade mayor to raise Pride flag at Stephen P. Clark Government Center (June 2021); continues annually w/ Progress Flag for second two weeks of June; issued Wynwood Pride proclamation (Miami-Dade.gov). (5) family_child_sovereignty q1 FALSE — condemned FL Parental Rights in Education Act; called LGBTQ History Month non-recognition 'weaponizing parental rights laws' (NBC Miami). (6) family_child_sovereignty q2 FALSE — called 'Don't Say Gay' bill 'cruel and ignorant'; met w/ students traveling to Capitol to protest (FL Politics). (7) refuse_state_overreach q0/q2/q3 FALSE — imposed indoor mask mandate at county facilities defying DeSantis EO; reinstated mask rule Dec 2021; urged vaccination publicly (FL Politics, News4Jax). (8) refuse_state_overreach q1 FALSE — pro-ESG posture: first-in-world Chief Heat Officer, Chief Resilience Officer, Climate Action Strategy, $2.5B '305 Future Ready' climate bond, electric bus fleet (Miami-Dade.gov resilience). (9) self_defense q0/q1 FALSE — joined Biden at White House celebrating 'historic gun legislation' with Parkland families July 2022 (CBS Miami). DEVIATION-FLAG: police budget. Increased police budget every year of first term; proposed +9% boost FY25-26 ($402M deficit context); said 'total public safety budget is not being cut' (WLRN July 2025) — atypical for progressive Dem, but new Miami-Dade Sheriff says her funding falls $40M short — so does NOT meet HIGH BAR for public_justice q0 affirmative pro-police advocacy; score null. DEVIATION-FLAG: 287(g) compliance — administration applied 287(g) Sept 2022 and signed updated ICE transport MOA 2025, BUT advocates characterize as state-compelled compliance and federal judge ruled county breached 2021 settlement protecting undocumented detainees (WLRN); compliance-without-enthusiasm, NOT affirmative anti-sanctuary advocacy — border cells null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on personhood, marriage definition, school choice (outside mayoral remit), christian_liberty cells (Jewish; no anti-Christian-display action found → null), election integrity, public_justice q1-q4 (no specific record), self_defense q2 — those null.

0.note: Publicly campaigned for FL Amendment 4 abortion-rights ballot measure at 'Our Bodies Our Lives' rally — explicit anti-personhood framing.

1.note: As mayoral candidate 'strongly opposed' legislation that would require minors to gain parental consent before an abortion.

2.note: At Pride flag-raising said 'We say gay. We say trans.'; launched county Community ID program serving transgender residents — explicit affirmation of trans identity policy.

4.note: First Miami-Dade mayor to raise Pride flag at Stephen P. Clark Government Center (June 2021); annual ceremony continued with Progress Flag in 2025; issued Wynwood Pride proclamation.

1.note: Condemned FL Parental Rights in Education Act; called school board's non-recognition of LGBTQ History Month 'weaponizing parental rights laws in an attempt to eradicate equality.'

2.note: Called 'Don't Say Gay' bill 'cruel and ignorant'; met with students traveling to FL Capitol to protest SOGI restrictions in K-12.

0.note: Imposed indoor mask mandate at county facilities defying DeSantis EO; reinstated mask rule Dec 2021 — imposed mandate, opposite of refusing overreach.

1.note: Pro-ESG posture: first-in-world Chief Heat Officer, Chief Resilience Officer, Climate Action Strategy, $2.5B '305 Future Ready' climate bond, electric bus fleet, 30% tree-canopy target.

2.note: Reinstated indoor mask rule at all county sites Dec 2021 — imposed mandate on citizens, opposite of protecting from mandates.

0.note: Joined Biden at White House July 2022 celebrating 'historic gun legislation' with Parkland families; posted 'most gun owners support [gun control] policies' — opposite of constitutional carry.

1.note: White House gun-legislation celebration explicitly supported Bipartisan Safer Communities Act incentives for state red-flag laws — opposite of anti-red-flag.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of church affiliation or denomination in public bios (Wikipedia, sao office bio, news profiles silent)
profile.education: BA + MA Criminology, University of Miami; LL.B., University of Cambridge; Miami Palmetto Senior HS
profile.background: State Attorney, 11th Judicial Circuit (Miami-Dade), Democrat. In office since March 12, 1993 — succeeded Janet Reno; longest-serving FL state attorney (~33 years). Re-elected unopposed in 2024; sworn for 7th term Jan 7, 2025; next election 2028. First Cuban-American/Hispanic female SA in FL; FL Women's Hall of Fame 2017. Born March 1, 1950, Washington, D.C.

katherine-fernandez-rundle.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Miami-Dade SA11 since March 1993 (succeeded Janet Reno); re-elected unopposed 2024; sworn 7th term Jan 7, 2025; next election 2028 (FL Politics, Ballotpedia). HARD evidence: (1) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — office posted 'A Message from State Attorney Katherine Fernandez Rundle during Pride Month' (Facebook video); runs LGBTQ hate-crimes partnership with FIU (affirmative Pride engagement). (2) election_integrity q3 TRUE — substantive prosecution record: convicted Frank Artiles in SD-37 ghost-candidate scheme (paid Alex Rodriguez ~$45K to confuse voters); charged non-citizen Lazaro Valle-Villar with 4 voting felonies (April 2024); activated Voter Protection Hotline/Task Force. Unusual for blue-county Dem SA. (3) public_justice q3 FALSE — embraces public-health framing: operates Drug Court, Mental Health Court, Truancy Intervention, 'Second Chance' expungement program, brands 'Smart Justice.' (4) self_defense q0 FALSE — actively HOSTILE: filed amicus arguing FL 2017 Stand Your Ground amendment is UNCONSTITUTIONAL before FL Supreme Court (Tampa Bay Times). DEVIATION-FLAG: 30+ years of NOT charging police in fatal on-duty shootings (Darren Rainey case is flagship — guards locked schizophrenic prisoner in 180°F shower 2 hrs, no charges; Miami-Dade Dem Party passed resolution demanding her resignation June 2017). This is institutional police deference, NOT 'affirmative defense of rightful use of force' — does NOT meet HIGH BAR for public_justice q0; score null, not TRUE. DEVIATION-FLAG: stopped prosecuting low-level marijuana possession in 2019; refused to publicly commit to enforcing FL 6-week abortion ban (FL Bulldog), BUT did not sign Fair & Just Prosecution non-enforcement pledge either — pure ambiguity, null on sanctity_of_life. Adopted no-cash bail for low-level offenses Jan 2020 but publicly opposed Chief Judge Sayfie's broader bail-reform plan ('stay intact') — MIXED, null on public_justice q2. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on personhood, marriage definition, trans-specific, school choice / parental notification / SOGI K-12, christian_liberty cells, economic stewardship q2, refuse_state_overreach, border (no clear policy stance on 287(g) or sanctuary cooperation), self_defense q1-q4 — those null.

4.note: Office posted 'A Message from State Attorney Katherine Fernandez Rundle during Pride Month' on Facebook; runs LGBTQ hate-crimes partnership with FIU — affirmative Pride engagement from official office.

3.note: Prosecuted Frank Artiles ghost-candidate scheme to conviction (paid Alex Rodriguez ~$45K to confuse SD-37 voters); charged non-citizen Lazaro Valle-Villar w/ 4 voting felonies 2024; activated Voter Protection Hotline/Task Force — substantive election-fraud prosecution record.

Operates Drug Court, Mental Health Court, Truancy Intervention, 'Second Chance' expungement program; brands 'Smart Justice'; stopped prosecuting low-level marijuana possession 2019 — embraces public-health/social-services framing for crime.

0.note: Filed amicus before FL Supreme Court arguing the 2017 Stand Your Ground amendment is UNCONSTITUTIONAL — hostile to the foundational FL self-defense framework.

profile.religion: NO direct personal profession of Christian faith found; inaugural ceremony notably featured Rabbi Mordechai Palgon and MDSO chief chaplain Rabbi Rosenberg; condemned Oct 7 attacks and antisemitism (Vin News)
profile.education: BS Criminal Justice, Marist College; MPA, Florida International University; FBI National Academy Session 265; FBI National Executive Institute Session 46
profile.background: Sheriff of Miami-Dade County, Republican. First elected Miami-Dade Sheriff in ~60 years under restored FL constitutional sheriff system; sworn in Jan 7, 2025. 4-year term to Jan 2029. Won Nov 2024 with 55.7% (563,828 votes) defeating Dem James Reyes. Career: MDPD officer 1996-2024, rose to Assistant Director (2022). Born Dominican Republic ~1969-70, raised by single mother in NYC. Trump 'Complete and Total Endorsement' April 24, 2024.

rosie-cordero-stutz.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Miami-Dade Sheriff (first elected in ~60 years), sworn Jan 7, 2025, term to Jan 2029 (FL Sheriffs Assn, Wikipedia, Local 10 Feb 2026 1-on-1). HARD evidence: (1) economic_stewardship q2 TRUE — publicly fought for full LEO funding, called shortfall 'defunding the police'; secured $30.8M additional after pushback; 'You can't put a price on public safety' (Local 10 July 2025). (2) public_justice q0 TRUE (HIGH BAR met) — career 28+ yr MDPD officer; publicly champions MDSO funding, staffing, anti-defund framing; endorsed by FOP, IUPA, HPOA. (3) public_justice q4 TRUE — 'My deputies will arrest anyone who has committed a crime, regardless of their immigration status' (CBS Miami) — affirmative anti-soft-on-crime. (4) border_immigration q0 TRUE — Miami-Dade signed 287(g) MOA under her, announced Feb 26-27, 2025 at Homestead with DeSantis; all 67 FL sheriffs signed; deputies undergoing 40-hr ICE training (WLRN, NBC Miami). (5) border_immigration q1 TRUE — 'If you are arrested for committing a crime and already have an immigration detainer, you will be turned over to the appropriate agency' (CBS Miami). (6) border_immigration q2 TRUE — 'We continue to support our federal partners when it comes to immigration, as we have done for many years'; joined statewide ICE partnership (WLRN). DEVIATION-FLAG: she explicitly names 'the mental health crisis' as one of three top priorities and partners with Homeless Trust + Mothers Fighting For Justice (Miami Times) — soft-on-cause public-health frame cuts against public_justice q3 → null/FALSE-leaning. NO Christian profession found; inaugural was notably Jewish-flavored — christian_liberty cells null, not TRUE. PBA split: largest state PBA endorsed her Dem opponent — only FOP/IUPA/HPOA backed her. No public 2A statements found despite Republican identification — surprising; self_defense cells null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage, family_child_sovereignty, christian_liberty, election_integrity, refuse_state_overreach, border q3 (illegal employment), self_defense q0-q4 — those null.

2.note: Publicly fought for full LEO funding; called proposed shortfall 'defunding the police'; secured $30.8M additional after public pushback at commission. 'You can't put a price on public safety.'

0.note: Career 28+ yr MDPD officer; publicly champions MDSO funding, staffing, anti-defund framing; FOP, IUPA, HPOA endorsements — affirmative pro-police advocacy meets HIGH BAR.

4.note: 'My deputies will arrest anyone who has committed a crime, regardless of their immigration status' — affirmative tough-on-crime posture, anti-soft-on-crime.

1.note: 'If you are arrested for committing a crime and already have an immigration detainer, you will be turned over to the appropriate agency' — affirmative cooperation w/ mandatory deportation of criminal aliens; signed 287(g) MOA Feb 2025 at Homestead w/ DeSantis.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of religion or denomination in sao9.net bio, Wikipedia, Ballotpedia, or news profiles
profile.education: BA St. John's University 1995; JD University of Florida Levin College of Law 2000
profile.background: State Attorney, 9th Judicial Circuit (Orange + Osceola Counties), Democrat. Elected 2020; SUSPENDED by Gov. DeSantis Aug 9, 2023 via EO 23-160 over charging policies (FL Supreme Court upheld 5-2 on June 6, 2024); replaced by Andrew Bain. RE-ELECTED Nov 5, 2024 with 57.5% defeating Bain; resumed office Jan 7, 2025. Current term to Jan 2029. Prior: Public Defender (Orange Co), private practice, UF clinical law professor, Founding Director Conviction Integrity Unit. Open Soros-network funding via Democracy PAC + FL Rights Restoration Coalition (~$1.5M 2020); Real Justice PAC endorsement.

monique-worrell.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: 9th Judicial Circuit SA, re-elected Nov 2024 (57.5% vs DeSantis-appointed Bain) after Aug 2023 DeSantis suspension upheld 5-2 by FL Supreme Court June 6, 2024; resumed office Jan 7, 2025; term to Jan 2029 (Wikipedia, Ballotpedia, FindLaw caselaw, ClickOrlando April 2026). HARD evidence: (1) sanctity_of_life q0 FALSE — on FL 6-week ban: 'This law is more than just a restriction; it's a dangerous intrusion into our private lives… #VoteYesOn4' (own Instagram reel). (2) sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE — same statement signals non-enforcement intent; pattern mirrors Warren predecessor (The Intercept). (3) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — declined to prosecute activists who restored Pulse rainbow crosswalk with chalk April 2026: 'This office will not use the power of prosecution as an instrument of political enforcement' (Spectrum News 13). (4) public_justice q0 FALSE — DeSantis EO 23-160 cited that her office 'prevented or discouraged' prosecutors from seeking minimum mandatory sentences for gun crimes and drug trafficking; FL FOP Lodge 25: 'Monique Worrell's soft on crime stance has yet again let the citizens of Orlando down' (Orlando Weekly Aug 2023). (5) public_justice q3 FALSE — on juveniles: 'we need to recognize that they are children' — reform framing over punitive (WFTV). (6) public_justice q4 FALSE — DeSantis suspended her specifically for soft-on-crime, 'neglect of duty and incompetence'; AG Uthmeier renewed attack May 2026 over 'sweetheart deals' (Tampa Bay Times). DEVIATION-FLAG: very rare case where a sitting state attorney has been formally adjudicated as 'neglect of duty' by an executive EO and upheld by the state supreme court — that's evidentiary-grade documentation of soft-on-crime charging policy. Re-elected after suspension w/ 57.5% — voters reaffirmed reform agenda. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on marriage definition (q0), trans (q2), family_child_sovereignty cells, christian_liberty cells, economic stewardship q2, election integrity q3 (no fraud-prosecution record located), public_justice q1/q2 (deflects bail decisions to court), refuse_state_overreach (her conflict with FL state is leftward), border (AG Uthmeier cited sanctuary-policies context but no specific anti-ICE quote found), self_defense — those cells null.

0.note: On FL 6-week ban: 'This law is more than just a restriction; it's a dangerous intrusion into our private lives… #VoteYesOn4' — explicit anti-personhood framing.

1.note: Vote Yes On 4 advocacy + The Intercept's documentation signal non-enforcement intent on FL abortion law — opposite of abolition.

4.note: Declined to prosecute LGBT activists arrested for chalking rainbow crosswalk near Pulse (April 2026): 'This office will not use the power of prosecution as an instrument of political enforcement.'

0.note: DeSantis EO 23-160 (upheld 5-2 by FL Supreme Court June 6, 2024) cited her office 'prevented or discouraged' prosecutors from seeking minimum mandatory sentences for gun crimes and drug trafficking; FL FOP Lodge 25 publicly accused her of 'soft on crime stance.'

3.note: On juvenile crime: 'we need to recognize that they are children' — embraces reform/diversion framing over punitive rule-of-law framing.
